text:
for partition was filed without contesting the sale deed, that itself 
was legally untenable. Moreover, a registered sale deed constitutes 
constructive notice to the world unless it is a case of fraud, coercion, 
or minority and therefore there has to be a presumption in law that 
the plaintiffs had knowledge of the sale deed.
[2025] 4 S.C.R.  
525
Smt. Uma Devi and Ors. v. Sri. Anand Kumar and Ors.
10. The Trial Court, considering these facts, allowed the application 
under Order 7 Rule 11 CPC and dismissed the suit, finding no cause 
of action for filing the suit. However, the appellate court found that 
there were triable issues that required consideration. The appellate 
court was of the opinion that the plaintiffs had a legitimate claim over 
the joint family properties, and in the absence of any notice to the 
plaintiffs regarding the partition, the suit was remanded back to the 
Trial Court for fresh consideration.
